title: RAG for Science: Using Retrieval-Augmented Generation to Navigate Research Literature
subtitle: Why LLMs alone are not enough for rigorous scientific synthesis — and how retrieval changes the equation
description: Retrieval-Augmented Generation grounds language model outputs in verifiable sources, making it a powerful tool for scientific literature review, hypothesis formation, and reproducible research workflows.
excerpt: An LLM trained on a static corpus cannot know what was published last month. RAG fixes that — by retrieving relevant papers at query time and grounding every claim in a traceable source.
hero_caption: A retrieval-augmented pipeline grounding LLM responses in a live corpus of scientific papers.
hero_aria: Diagram of a RAG pipeline retrieving documents from a vector store to augment an LLM scientific query
